cards.title: Bioluminescent
cards.description: I was going for a comet. The paint had other ideas. Azteca Ultramarine Blue as ground and top layer, metallic blues with silicone oil in between, Dutch pour with a hairdryer and straw. The result was biological rather than astronomical.

cards.title: Across the room.
cards.description: I had a shower curtain I loved. I decided to make the painting that went with it. Swipe pour with a wet paper towel — one pass per zone. They read the same from across the room.

cards.title: Spacey, not oceanic.
cards.description: Same paint, same session as Bioluminescent. Leftover cups flipped onto a round canvas. The round format removed the horizon line — without a horizon, the same palette stopped reading as ocean and started reading as space.

cards.title: Darkness emerging from light.
cards.description: An inversion of the usual ground. White base, blues and greens on top. White is the heaviest pigment and sinks through the lighter colors. Air from a blow dryer opens the cells. On a cigar box lid — dense, compressed, nothing has room to drift.

cards.title: The fold-down shelf.
cards.description: When the bracket is flat, it's a painting. When it's open, it's a shelf. Two flip cups to cover the full length, countersunk screw holes drilled before pouring. Bolted into studs. Holds a speaker, a pen cup, hand lotion. Occasionally a cat.

cards.title: The skin remembers.
cards.description: A pour happens once. The skin carries it somewhere else. Two glass vessels — one wrapped in a skin from the runoff of a painting that no longer exists, one from an intentional pour. Mod Podge, clear coat, pristine glass inside. Still functional.

cards.title: The candle was first.
cards.description: The paint was mixed for a glass panel project that didn't work out. The leftover fell on the plastic sheeting, pooled, and dried into something worth keeping. Ultramarine Blue mixed thin for transparency — the same transparency that makes it extraordinary when light passes through it.

cards.title: The bottle depicts what it does.
cards.description: The incense I burn produces smoke that falls rather than rises. The bottle needed to show it. Green pour standing upright — paint falling the way smoke falls. Black and silver skin wrapped over it, the cellular structure reading exactly like smoke caught in a photograph.

cards.title: The design tool is rotation.
cards.description: Not gravity. Not air. Centrifugal force. A promotional thermal cup on a cup turner — resin and mica applied while it spins, each color settling into its own orbit. Silver, purple, blue, teal, olive. First time working with resin and mica.

cards.title: The lamp it was supposed to be.
cards.description: Wood base, wicker shade. Neither wrong — just not right. Stripped the seagrass, rebuilt the shade using a rainbow arc tile pattern designed in GIMP, printed, laminated, and fitted to the frame. The base repainted in iridescent copper and rose. Not a quiet object.

cards.title: Pax et Amor
cards.description: The box is packing material. The panels are oiled paper cut and assembled to behave like stained glass when backlit. The image was AI-generated. Inside, a light strip on a timer.

cards.title: It looks like cast iron.
cards.description: It is cardboard. Four to five layers laminated with wheat paste, paper mached smooth, three-legged form. The top is a fluid pour in black and white — it reads as volcanic stone. Sits outside the front door through Portland weather. First in a series.

cards.title: The Pax Box
cards.description: A side table designed to look like the cat who uses it. Paint skin lid sealed with resin, black felt collar band with silver upholstery tacks, two cat-proof latches, motion-activated interior light. Stores cat toys and catnip. The catnip is the issue.

cards.title: Pax house
cards.description: Fully detached. One occupant. Mail delivery available. Built entirely from reclaimed materials — cardboard A-frame, hand-cut shingles, glass bead texture on all blue surfaces, scratch-built mailbox with a working flag. Interior waterproofed and fully felt-lined.
